meta.title: Multi-Tenant Telemetry Ingestion
meta.description: Events from multiple tenants are sampled or rejected by a shared telemetry collector. Accepted EU events flush to the observability platform; Tenant D's events are cross-region-forwarded to an external cloud provider outside euwest-1.
SaaS tenant A. Emits metrics, logs, and traces via OTLP/gRPC.
SaaS tenant B. Mixed signal types. Frequently sends debug-tagged events.
meta.notes: Debug tag filtering applies to this tenant.

meta.description: SaaS tenant C. Primarily distributed trace spans.
SaaS tenant D. US-headquartered. Data residency agreement requires all events to be forwarded to their dedicated AWS us-east-1 pipeline — not stored in the shared EU observability platform.
OpenTelemetry Collector. Applies tag-based sampling, PII filtering, cardinality caps, and per-tenant routing before forwarding accepted events.
meta.notes: Rules: drop debug=true, drop pii_detected=true, cap 10k series/tenant, route data_residency=us-east-1 to conn_ext.

meta.description: Grafana + Tempo + Loki stack. Stores accepted metrics, traces, and logs for EU tenants.
AWS Kinesis Data Firehose in us-east-1. Receives Tenant D's telemetry and delivers it to their dedicated S3 + Athena stack.

steps.name: Overview
steps.title: Multi-tenant telemetry pipeline
steps.description: Four tenants stream events to a shared OpenTelemetry Collector inside euwest-1. The collector applies tag-based sampling and routing rules. EU tenants (A, B, C) flush accepted events to the observability platform. Tenant D has a data residency requirement — its events bypass the EU store and are forwarded cross-region to AWS Kinesis Firehose in us-east-1.

steps.name: A: metric accepted
steps.title: Tenant A — metric event ingested
steps.description: Tenant A emits a checkout latency metric with standard production tags. No restricted tags detected; the collector samples it at 100% and queues it for the next EU batch export.

annotations.text: ACCEPT — sampled at 100%, queued for EU batch export

steps.name: B: debug tag dropped
steps.title: Tenant B — debug=true event rejected
steps.description: Tenant B sends a log event with debug=true in the tag set. The collector's sampling policy blocks all debug-tagged events in the production pipeline.

annotations.text: REJECT — tag debug=true is blocked in prod pipeline

data.body: Auth token refreshed for user u_82fa

steps.name: C: trace sampled
steps.title: Tenant C — trace span accepted at 10%
steps.description: Tenant C emits a distributed trace span for an outbound API call. The collector applies 10% head-based sampling for trace signals from this tenant. This span wins the sample lottery and is accepted.

annotations.text: ACCEPT — won 10% head-based sample, queued for EU batch export

steps.name: A: PII tag dropped
steps.title: Tenant A — pii_detected=true event rejected
steps.description: Tenant A sends a log line flagged with pii_detected=true. The collector's compliance filter drops any event carrying this tag before it can reach the shared observability store.

steps.name: B: cardinality capped
steps.title: Tenant B — metric series over cardinality cap
steps.description: Tenant B's instrumentation emits a metric with an unbounded user_id dimension, pushing its unique series count past the 10k/tenant cap. The collector drops the metric and records a quota violation.

steps.name: D: routed externally
steps.title: Tenant D — event accepted, routing to aws-us-east-1
steps.description: Tenant D sends a metric event. Its payload carries data_residency=us-east-1, which matches the collector's cross-region routing rule. The event passes all filters but is placed in the external queue rather than the EU batch — it will never touch the shared observability platform.

steps.name: D: cross-region flush
steps.title: Collector forwards Tenant D batch to Kinesis Firehose
steps.description: The collector delivers Tenant D's accumulated events over a TLS connection to AWS Kinesis Data Firehose in us-east-1. The data crosses the euwest-1 boundary and lands in Tenant D's private delivery stream, completely isolated from the EU observability stack.

annotations.text: DELIVERED — 612 events landed in tenant-d-telemetry stream

steps.name: EU flush
steps.title: Collector flushes EU accepted batch
steps.description: Every 30 seconds the collector compresses and forwards the accumulated EU-accepted events to the observability platform. Tenant D's events were already shipped cross-region. The three rejected events were permanently discarded at the collector.
